changes.before: She had made the same warning three times: once for a border lieutenant, once for a senior scout, once for the dark-braided ranger who had taken her satchel with careful hands and enough sense to know what the instruments were.
changes.after: She had made the same warning three times. Once for a border lieutenant, once for a senior scout, once for the dark-braided ranger who had taken her satchel with careful hands and enough sense to know what the instruments were.

changes.before: The ward-light climbed the threshold.
changes.after: Ward-light spread across the threshold.

changes.before: Her eyes swept the room once.
changes.after: She swept the room once.

changes.before: Farstrider armor worn the way a second skin is worn: no settling tug, no show of rank, no wasted motion.
changes.after: Farstrider armor worn the way a second skin is worn, with no settling tug, no show of rank, and no wasted motion.

changes.before: Jaina's hands wanted to map the pattern in the air.
changes.after: Jaina wanted to map the pattern in the air and let her hands do it.

changes.before: She heard herself doing it again: careful, pared down, built for someone she assumed needed the simpler version.
changes.after: She heard herself doing it again—careful, pared down, built for someone she assumed needed the simpler version.

changes.before: This was no courtesy glance.
changes.after: She tracked the route without hesitation, weighing roads, villages, and crossings in the same sweep.

changes.before: Jaina watched her eyes track the route without hesitation.
changes.after: She tracked the route without hesitation, weighing roads, villages, and crossings in the same sweep.

changes.before: This was no courtesy glance.
changes.after: She tracked the route without hesitation, weighing roads, villages, and crossings in the same sweep.

changes.before: The read hit harder than the last one: deeper, uglier, faster.
changes.after: The read hit harder than the last one, deeper and uglier and faster.

changes.before: Cyndia's eyes went to the tree line.
changes.after: Cyndia turned to the tree line.

changes.before: The horses felt it too.
changes.after: Removed; the concrete horse reaction remains in the next sentence.

changes.before: It was not just passing through. It was taking hold.
changes.after: It was taking hold.

changes.before: Millhaven is where it bunches hard enough to show its hand.
changes.after: Millhaven is where it bunches hard enough to become visible.

changes.before: Jaina could see it from the road now: soft heads of grain bending too low, leaves gone dark at the edges, damp ground under rows that should have been dry.
changes.after: Soft heads of grain bent too low at the road's edge, leaves gone dark at the edges, damp ground under rows that should have been dry.

changes.before: Rot had reached the crops.
changes.after: Soft heads of grain bent too low at the road's edge, leaves gone dark at the edges, damp ground under rows that should have been dry.

changes.before: Jaina could see it from the road now: soft heads of grain bending too low, leaves gone dark at the edges, damp ground under rows that should have been dry.
changes.after: Soft heads of grain bent too low at the road's edge, leaves gone dark at the edges, damp ground under rows that should have been dry.

changes.before: The corruption had gotten into the land itself, working through the crops and the soil.
changes.after: Removed; the image sentence now carries the beat without summary closure.

changes.before: Then the village showed through the last of the trees: cold hearths, no smoke, stillness where evening life should have been.
changes.after: Then the village showed through the last of the trees, cold hearths, no smoke, stillness where evening life should have been.

changes.before: The smell coming off the carcasses was not ordinary blood. It carried that same sweet-rot the wind had been dragging down the road.
changes.after: The smell coming off the carcasses carried that same sweet-rot the wind had been dragging down the road.

changes.before: The worst part was the magic in living flesh, keeping it going after sense and mercy had both run out.
changes.after: Removed; the prior sentence already carries the living-flesh implication.

changes.before: They didn't die first and rise after. It got into them while they were alive and kept driving them when their bodies should have failed.
changes.after: It got into them while they were alive and kept driving them when their bodies should have failed.

changes.before: Millhaven gathered out of the dusk by a river bend: low buildings, a still mill wheel, cold chimneys, no light anywhere.
changes.after: Millhaven appeared out of the dusk by a river bend with low buildings, a still mill wheel, cold chimneys, and no light anywhere.

changes.before: Millhaven gathered out of the dusk by a river bend: low buildings, a still mill wheel, cold chimneys, no light anywhere.
changes.after: Millhaven appeared out of the dusk by a river bend with low buildings, a still mill wheel, cold chimneys, and no light anywhere.

changes.before: She had been right about the spread. Right about the signature. Right enough to cross a border alone and ride into this.
changes.after: Removed; the scene now ends on Jaina gripping the staff as the village comes up at them.
